2017-08-26.abstract: Chinese investments in Greece are beginning to pay off, not only economically, but also by providing Beijing with a seemingly reliable ally in Brussels.

2017-08-26.abstract: Pyongyang fired three rockets capable of hitting U.S. bases in South Korea. Two of them traveled 155 miles before splashing down.

2017-08-26.title: Where They Weigh Scorpions
2017-08-26.abstract: It's the annual weigh-in at the London Zoo, where keepers take the vital statistics of hundreds of species to help with their care and well being, and to help with conservation efforts.

2017-08-26.title: Morocco’s Stability Is Roiled by Monthslong Protests Over Fishmonger’s Death
2017-08-26.abstract: What started as a spontaneous call for a serious inquiry into a vendor’s death in a Berber area has turned into one of the longest protests in North Africa since the Arab Spring.

2017-08-26.title: Funeral for Teenager Killed by Philippine Police Galvanizes Duterte Critics
2017-08-26.abstract: Thousands of Filipinos joined a march on Saturday for a 17-year-old boy whose death has fueled opposition to the president’s brutal war on drugs.

2017-08-27.title: Young Yemeni Girl Is Sole Survivor After Airstrike Topples Her Home
2017-08-27.abstract: Buthaina Muhammad Mansour, believed to be 4 or 5, lost eight members of her family in the attack in Sana, Yemen’s capital.

2017-08-27.title: Lebanese Army, Hezbollah and Syrian Army Declare Cease-Fire With ISIS
2017-08-27.abstract: The Lebanese Army announced a truce to try to recover soldiers. Separately, Hezbollah and its Syrian allies said Islamic State fighters had surrendered.

2017-08-27.title: Iran Out to Remake Mideast With Arab Enforcer: Hezbollah
2017-08-27.abstract: The “Party of God” has evolved from a Lebanese militant group into the spearhead of an international militia network led by Tehran.

2017-08-27.title: Thailand’s Ex-Leader Leaves Supporters in Limbo After Disappearing During Trial
2017-08-27.abstract: Yingluck Shinawatra, the former prime minister, is believed to have gone into exile rather than face a possible 10 years in prison on negligence charges.

2017-08-27.title: Argentine Paper Stood Up to the Generals, but Succumbed to Market Forces
2017-08-27.abstract: The Buenos Aires Herald, which exposed abuse during the 1976-83 military dictatorship, published its final edition last month.

2017-08-28.title: Remains of 10 Sailors Who Died in Navy Collision Are Found
2017-08-28.abstract: The remains were recovered in the crushed and flooded compartments of the destroyer John S. McCain a week after it collided with an oil tanker near Singapore.

2017-08-28.title: Ahead of U.N.’s Child Rights Blacklist, Saudis Stress Their Aid to Yemen
2017-08-28.abstract: The leader of the United Nations denied that the organization faced pressure from Saudi Arabia to exclude it from an annual list of countries that kill children in war.

2017-08-28.title: Australia’s Immigration Chief Defends Cutting Support for Some Asylum Seekers
2017-08-28.abstract: Peter Dutton said that asylum seekers who had been allowed into Australia for medical treatment needed to be compelled to return to offshore detention centers.

2017-08-28.title: China and India Agree to Ease Tensions in Border Dispute
2017-08-28.abstract: Analysts say that both sides gave ground, ending one of the worst diplomatic flare-ups between the nuclear-armed Asian rivals in decades.

2017-08-28.title: Indian Guru Whose Followers Rioted Is Sentenced for Rape
2017-08-28.abstract: The guru, Gurmeet Ram Rahim Singh, was found guilty of having raped two women more than a decade ago. He was sentenced to 20 years in prison.

2017-08-29.title: Iran Upholds Convictions of Iranian-American Father and Son
2017-08-29.abstract: The lawyer for Baquer and Siamak Namazi denounced the “cruel and unjust” decision, which could still further worsen relations with Iran.

2017-08-29.title: Japan Wakes to a Text Message: Missile Approaching
2017-08-29.abstract: An alert that roused people from sleep and interrupted the morning commute warned residents that North Korea had launched a missile.

2017-08-29.title: The Empire Stopper
2017-08-29.abstract: The foreign powers that have tried to control Afghanistan since the 19th century have all suffered for the effort. Now the U.S. is digging back in.

2017-08-29.title: A Pacifist Japan Starts to Embrace the Military
2017-08-29.abstract: As the North Korean missile threat grows, there are signs that Japan’s citizens are moving away from the country’s postwar pacifism.

2017-08-29.title: Australian State Removes Racially Offensive Place Names
2017-08-29.abstract: The names of several mountains and creeks in Queensland containing racial slurs have been removed from the state’s records, the latest such move in a country still reckoning with its colonial past.

2017-08-30.title: Guatemala’s Top Court Says President Can’t Expel Chief of Anticorruption Panel
2017-08-30.abstract: The Constitutional Court ruled that President Jimmy Morales could not remove the commissioner of a United Nations panel that has accused him of violating campaign finance laws.

2017-08-30.title: In Memories of Diana, Princess of Wales, a Vast Generational Divide
2017-08-30.abstract: She is considered a towering symbol of her era for older Britons. But for young adults, as one teenager said, “she’s not really an icon.”

2017-08-30.title: A Scion of Mexico Fights Corruption, and Becomes a Target
2017-08-30.abstract: The son of a corporate chairman has been targeted by advanced spying technology bought by the Mexican government to combat terrorism and organized crime.

2017-08-30.title: After Missile Tests, North and South Korea Wage War of Pictures
2017-08-30.abstract: South Korea appeared not to want to be outdone after North Korea’s recent missile test, releasing footage of a missile that Seoul launched the previous week.

2017-08-30.title: Former South Korean Spy Chief Sentenced for Trying to Sway Election
2017-08-30.abstract: Won Sei-hoon was sentenced to four years in prison for ordering an online smear campaign to help Park Geun-hye get elected president in 2012.

2017-08-31.title: U.S. Gives Military Assistance to Pakistan, With Strings Attached
2017-08-31.abstract: The $255 million in military aid will be put into escrow. Unlocking the funds will require Islamabad to stop supporting the Taliban.

2017-08-31.title: U.N. Peacekeepers in Lebanon Get Stronger Inspection Powers for Hezbollah Arms
2017-08-31.abstract: The United States had complained that the force patrolling the Lebanon-Israel border was not preventing Hezbollah from amassing and hiding weapons.

2017-08-31.title: Death of Diana: Times Journalists Recall Night of the Crash
2017-08-31.abstract: Five New York Times journalists remember what it was like to report 20 years ago on the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, and her life.

2017-08-31.title: Diana’s Legacy: A Reshaped Monarchy, a More Emotional U.K.
2017-08-31.abstract: Twenty years after her death, Diana’s influence can still be felt in unexpected ways in Britain.

2017-08-31.title: Diana’s Public Life, in Photos and Headlines
2017-08-31.abstract: With Britain commemorating the 20th anniversary of the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, we looked through archival photographs and our own pages to remember her life.
